---
title: "Publications"
---

# Publications

This page is organised by research field. The list below includes verified and publicly documented publications and can be expanded from a Google Scholar BibTeX export without changing the website design.

## Health preference research and choice modelling

<div class="pub"><div class="pub-field">Choice modelling · Kidney transplantation</div><div class="pub-title">The role of heterogeneity of patients' preferences in kidney transplantation</div><div class="pub-meta">Genie, Nicolo and Pasini · 2020 · <em>Journal of Health Economics</em> · DOI: 10.1016/j.jhealeco.2020.102331</div></div>

<div class="pub"><div class="pub-field">Choice modelling · Cost information</div><div class="pub-title">To pay or not to pay? Cost information processing in the valuation of publicly funded healthcare</div><div class="pub-meta">Genie, Ryan and Krucien · 2021 · <em>Social Science & Medicine</em> · DOI: 10.1016/j.socscimed.2021.113822</div></div>

<div class="pub"><div class="pub-field">Choice modelling · Eye tracking</div><div class="pub-title">Keeping an eye on cost: What can eye tracking tell us about attention to cost information in discrete choice experiments?</div><div class="pub-meta">Genie · 2023 · <em>Health Economics</em> · DOI: 10.1002/hec.4658</div></div>

<div class="pub"><div class="pub-field">Choice modelling · Decision quality</div><div class="pub-title">Choice Consistency in Discrete Choice Experiments: Does Numeracy Skill Matter?</div><div class="pub-meta">Genie et al. · 2024 · <em>Value in Health</em></div></div>

<div class="pub"><div class="pub-field">Value assessment · Equity</div><div class="pub-title">Priority for Self or Others? Incorporating Equity Considerations in a Preference-Based Health Value Assessment</div><div class="pub-meta">Genie et al. · 2025 · <em>Value in Health</em></div></div>

<div class="pub"><div class="pub-field">Choice modelling · Feedback</div><div class="pub-title">Guidance or Misdirection? Unpacking the Role of Feedback in Health Preference Assessments</div><div class="pub-meta">Genie, Reed and Ozdemir · 2026 · <em>Health Economics</em></div></div>

## Public health, vaccination and mandates

<div class="pub"><div class="pub-field">Pandemic policy · DCE protocol</div><div class="pub-title">Understanding public preferences and trade-offs for government responses during a pandemic: a protocol for a discrete choice experiment in the UK</div><div class="pub-meta">Genie et al. · 2020 · <em>BMJ Open</em> · DOI: 10.1136/bmjopen-2020-043477</div></div>

<div class="pub"><div class="pub-field">Vaccination · Evidence synthesis</div><div class="pub-title">Open repository of COVID-19 vaccine mandate studies and scoping review</div><div class="pub-meta">Genie et al. · 2026 · <em>npj Vaccines</em></div></div>

## Health policy, ageing and digital health

<div class="pub"><div class="pub-field">Ageing · Loneliness</div><div class="pub-title">Feeling lonely? Preferences for support programmes to reduce loneliness among older adults in Australia: A discrete choice experiment</div><div class="pub-meta">Genie, Boyes and Paolucci · 2026 · <em>Health Policy</em></div></div>

<div class="pub"><div class="pub-field">Health system utilisation</div><div class="pub-title">Immigration and the utilisation of preventive care in Europe: Results from retrospective data</div><div class="pub-meta">Buia, Genie and Pasini · 2026 · <em>Empirical Economics</em></div></div>

## Methods and robustness

<div class="pub"><div class="pub-field">DCE methods</div><div class="pub-title">Fit for Purpose? Assessing the Robustness of Discrete Choice Experiment Designs in the Context of Evolving Health Technologies</div><div class="pub-meta">Genie, Watson, Schwarzinger and Luchini · 2026 · <em>Social Science & Medicine</em></div></div>

## Publication maintenance

To add the complete Google Scholar list:

1. Open Google Scholar profile.
2. Select publications.
3. Export as BibTeX.
4. Save the file as `assets/publications.bib`.
5. Commit changes to GitHub.

The website is already styled to display publications by field; the `assets/publications_by_field.csv` file gives the structure for importing and classifying the full list.
